root_text.sn44.6:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ āyasmā ca sāriputto, āyasmā ca mahākoṭṭhiko bārāṇasiyaṁ viharanti isipatane migadāye.
root_text.sn44.6:1.2: Atha kho āyasmā sāriputto sāyanhasamayaṁ paṭisallānā vuṭṭhito yenāyasmā mahākoṭṭhiko tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmatā mahākoṭṭhikena saddhiṁ sammodi.
root_text.sn44.6:1.3: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho āyasmā sāriputto āyasmantaṁ mahākoṭṭhikaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn44.6:1.4: “‘kiṁ nu kho, āvuso koṭṭhika, hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’ti …pe…
root_text.sn44.6:1.5: ‘kiṁ panāvuso, neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’ti iti puṭṭho samāno:
root_text.sn44.6:1.7: neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’ti vadesi.
root_text.sn44.6:1.8: Ko nu kho, āvuso, hetu, ko paccayo, yenetaṁ abyākataṁ bhagavatā”ti?
root_text.sn44.6:2.1: “Rūpārāmassa kho, āvuso, rūparatassa rūpasammuditassa rūpanirodhaṁ ajānato apassato yathābhūtaṁ, ‘hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’tipissa hoti;
root_text.sn44.6:2.3: ‘hoti ca na ca hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’tipissa hoti;
root_text.sn44.6:2.4: ‘neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’tipissa hoti.
root_text.sn44.6:2.5: Vedanārāmassa kho, āvuso, vedanāratassa vedanāsammuditassa, vedanānirodhaṁ ajānato apassato yathābhūtaṁ, ‘hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’tipissa hoti …pe…
root_text.sn44.6:2.8: viññāṇārāmassa kho, āvuso, viññāṇaratassa viññāṇasammuditassa viññāṇanirodhaṁ ajānato apassato yathābhūtaṁ, ‘hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’tipissa hoti …pe…
root_text.sn44.6:2.9: ‘neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’tipissa hoti.
root_text.sn44.6:3.1: Na rūpārāmassa kho, āvuso, na rūparatassa na rūpasammuditassa, rūpanirodhaṁ jānato passato yathābhūtaṁ, ‘hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’tipissa na hoti …pe…
root_text.sn44.6:3.2: ‘neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’tipissa na hoti.
root_text.sn44.6:3.6: na viññāṇārāmassa kho, āvuso, na viññāṇaratassa na viññāṇasammuditassa, viññāṇanirodhaṁ jānato passato yathābhūtaṁ, ‘hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’tipissa na hoti …pe…
root_text.sn44.6:3.7: ‘neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’tipissa na hoti.
root_text.sn44.6:3.8: Ayaṁ kho, āvuso, hetu, ayaṁ paccayo, yenetaṁ abyākataṁ bhagavatā”ti.
root_text.sn44.6:4.1: “Siyā panāvuso, aññopi pariyāyo, yenetaṁ abyākataṁ bhagavatā”ti?
root_text.sn44.6:4.3: Bhavārāmassa kho, āvuso, bhavaratassa bhavasammuditassa, bhavanirodhaṁ ajānato apassato yathābhūtaṁ, ‘hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’tipissa hoti …pe…
root_text.sn44.6:4.4: ‘neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’tipissa hoti.
root_text.sn44.6:4.5: Na bhavārāmassa kho, āvuso, na bhavaratassa na bhavasammuditassa, bhavanirodhaṁ jānato passato yathābhūtaṁ, ‘hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’tipissa na hoti …pe…
root_text.sn44.6:4.6: ‘neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’tipissa na hoti.
root_text.sn44.6:4.7: Ayampi kho, āvuso, pariyāyo, yenetaṁ abyākataṁ bhagavatā”ti.
root_text.sn44.6:5.1: “Siyā panāvuso, aññopi pariyāyo, yenetaṁ abyākataṁ bhagavatā”ti?
root_text.sn44.6:5.3: Upādānārāmassa kho, āvuso, upādānaratassa upādānasammuditassa, upādānanirodhaṁ ajānato apassato yathābhūtaṁ, ‘hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’tipissa hoti …pe…
root_text.sn44.6:5.4: ‘neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’tipissa hoti.
root_text.sn44.6:5.5: Na upādānārāmassa kho, āvuso, na upādānaratassa na upādānasammuditassa, upādānanirodhaṁ jānato passato yathābhūtaṁ, ‘hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’tipissa na hoti …pe…
root_text.sn44.6:5.6: ‘neva, hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’tipissa na hoti.
root_text.sn44.6:5.7: Ayampi kho, āvuso, pariyāyo, yenetaṁ abyākataṁ bhagavatā”ti.
root_text.sn44.6:6.1: “Siyā panāvuso, aññopi pariyāyo, yenetaṁ abyākataṁ bhagavatā”ti?
root_text.sn44.6:6.3: Taṇhārāmassa kho, āvuso, taṇhāratassa taṇhāsammuditassa, taṇhānirodhaṁ ajānato apassato yathābhūtaṁ, ‘hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’tipissa hoti …pe…
root_text.sn44.6:6.4: ‘neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’tipissa hoti.
root_text.sn44.6:6.5: Na taṇhārāmassa kho, āvuso, na taṇhāratassa na taṇhāsammuditassa, taṇhānirodhaṁ jānato passato yathābhūtaṁ, ‘hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’tipissa na hoti …pe…
root_text.sn44.6:6.6: ‘neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’tipissa na hoti.
root_text.sn44.6:6.7: Ayampi kho, āvuso, pariyāyo, yenetaṁ abyākataṁ bhagavatā”ti.
root_text.sn44.6:7.1: “Siyā panāvuso, aññopi pariyāyo, yenetaṁ abyākataṁ bhagavatā”ti?
root_text.sn44.6:7.2: “Ettha dāni, āvuso sāriputta, ito uttari kiṁ icchasi?
root_text.sn44.6:7.3: Taṇhāsaṅkhayavimuttassa, āvuso sāriputta, bhikkhuno vaṭṭaṁ natthi paññāpanāyā”ti.

translation_text.sn44.6:1.1: At one time Venerable Sāriputta and Venerable Mahākoṭṭhita were staying near Varanasi, in the deer park at Isipatana.
translation_text.sn44.6:1.2: Then in the late afternoon, Venerable Sāriputta came out of retreat, went to Venerable Mahākoṭṭhita, and they greeted each other.
translation_text.sn44.6:1.3: When the greetings and polite conversation were over, he sat down to one side and said to Mahākoṭṭhita:
translation_text.sn44.6:1.4: “Reverend Koṭṭhita, does a realized one still exist after death?” …
translation_text.sn44.6:1.5: “Reverend, when asked these questions, you say that this has not been declared by the Buddha.
translation_text.sn44.6:1.8: What’s the cause, what’s the reason why this has not been declared by the Buddha?”
translation_text.sn44.6:2.1: “Reverend, if you like, love, and enjoy form, and don’t truly see the cessation of form, you think ‘a realized one still exists after death’ or
translation_text.sn44.6:2.2: ‘A realized one no longer exists after death’ or
translation_text.sn44.6:2.3: ‘a realized one both still exists and no longer exists after death’ or
translation_text.sn44.6:2.4: ‘a realized one neither still exists nor no longer exists after death.’
translation_text.sn44.6:2.5: If you like, love, and enjoy feeling …
translation_text.sn44.6:2.8: consciousness, and don’t truly see the cessation of consciousness, you think ‘a realized one still exists after death’ …
translation_text.sn44.6:2.9: ‘a realized one neither still exists nor no longer exists after death.’
translation_text.sn44.6:3.1: If you don’t like, love, and enjoy form …
translation_text.sn44.6:3.6: consciousness, and you truly see the cessation of consciousness, you don’t think ‘a realized one still exists after death’ …
translation_text.sn44.6:3.7: ‘a realized one neither still exists nor no longer exists after death.’
translation_text.sn44.6:3.8: This is the cause, this is the reason why this has not been declared by the Buddha.”
translation_text.sn44.6:4.1: “But reverend, could there be another way of explaining why this was not declared by the Buddha?”
translation_text.sn44.6:4.3: If you like, love, and enjoy existence, and don’t truly see the cessation of continued existence, you think ‘a realized one still exists after death’ …
translation_text.sn44.6:4.4: ‘a realized one neither still exists nor no longer exists after death.’
translation_text.sn44.6:4.5: If you don’t like, love, and enjoy existence, and you truly see the cessation of continued existence, you don’t think ‘a realized one still exists after death’ …
translation_text.sn44.6:4.6: ‘a realized one neither still exists nor no longer exists after death.’
translation_text.sn44.6:4.7: This too is a way of explaining why this was not declared by the Buddha.”
translation_text.sn44.6:5.1: “But reverend, could there be another way of explaining why this was not declared by the Buddha?”
translation_text.sn44.6:5.3: If you like, love, and enjoy grasping, and don’t truly see the cessation of grasping, you think ‘a realized one still exists after death’ …
translation_text.sn44.6:5.4: ‘a realized one neither still exists nor no longer exists after death.’
translation_text.sn44.6:5.5: If you don’t like, love, and enjoy grasping, and you truly see the cessation of grasping, you don’t think ‘a realized one still exists after death’ …
translation_text.sn44.6:5.6: ‘a realized one neither still exists nor no longer exists after death.’
translation_text.sn44.6:5.7: This too is a way of explaining why this was not declared by the Buddha.”
translation_text.sn44.6:6.1: “But reverend, could there be another way of explaining why this was not declared by the Buddha?”
translation_text.sn44.6:6.3: If you like, love, and enjoy craving, and don’t truly see the cessation of craving, you think ‘a realized one still exists after death’ …
translation_text.sn44.6:6.4: ‘a realized one neither still exists nor no longer exists after death.’
translation_text.sn44.6:6.5: If you don’t like, love, and enjoy craving, and you truly see the cessation of craving, you don’t think ‘a realized one still exists after death’ …
translation_text.sn44.6:6.6: ‘a realized one neither still exists nor no longer exists after death.’
translation_text.sn44.6:6.7: This too is a way of explaining why this was not declared by the Buddha.”
translation_text.sn44.6:7.1: “But reverend, could there be another way of explaining why this was not declared by the Buddha?”
translation_text.sn44.6:7.2: “Seriously, reverend, what more could you want?
translation_text.sn44.6:7.3: For one who is freed due to the ending of craving, there is no cycle of rebirths to be found.”
